title: Gratuities
summary: Government may terminate the contract if the contractor or its agents offered a gratuity to influence award. Bidders with active gratuity findings cannot proceed.

title: Covenant Against Contingent Fees
summary: Bidder warrants no contingent fee was paid to secure the contract (except bona-fide employees / agencies).

title: Contractor Code of Business Ethics and Conduct
summary: Contractors with awards >$6M and POP >120 days must maintain a written code of ethics, internal control system, and ethics training program.

title: Approval of Contract
summary: Contract not binding until signed by approving authority.

title: System for Award Management
summary: Offeror must be registered in SAM at proposal submission and through award. Active SAM registration is a hard prerequisite.

title: Annual Representations and Certifications
summary: Offeror's reps and certs in SAM are incorporated by reference. Reps must be current.

title: Reporting Executive Compensation and First-Tier Subcontract Awards
summary: Contractor must report first-tier subcontract awards >$30K and executive compensation in FFATA / FSRS.

title: System for Award Management Maintenance
summary: Contractor must maintain accurate SAM registration through life of contract.

title: Commercial and Government Entity Code Reporting
summary: Offeror must report CAGE code and any prior CAGE codes for affiliates.

title: Representation Regarding Certain Telecommunications and Video Surveillance Services or Equipment
summary: Section 889 prohibits use of covered Chinese telecom/surveillance equipment (Huawei, ZTE, Hikvision, Dahua, Hytera). Offeror must represent compliance.

title: Prohibition on Contracting for Certain Telecommunications and Video Surveillance Services or Equipment
summary: Government may not procure or extend a contract with any entity that uses covered telecom equipment as substantial component or critical technology.

title: Qualification Requirements
summary: When qualification requirements apply, offeror must demonstrate qualified product/service. Past performance must be relevant and recent.

title: Certification Regarding Responsibility Matters
summary: Offeror must certify it has not within the last 3 years been convicted of fraud, antitrust violations, tax evasion, or had a contract terminated for default.

title: Information Regarding Responsibility Matters
summary: Offerors with active federal contracts/grants totaling >$10M must disclose FAPIIS information including criminal proceedings, civil judgments, and terminations.

title: Instructions to Offerors—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: Standard instructions for commercial-item proposals: format, content, multiple awards, evaluation period.

title: Evaluation—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: Evaluation factors for commercial-item awards. Government identifies factors and relative importance.

title: Offeror Representations and Certifications—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: Comprehensive reps and certs covering size status, set-aside eligibility, ownership, debarment, foreign ownership, telecom restrictions.

title: Contract Terms and Conditions—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: Standard terms and conditions for commercial-item contracts. Replaces many uniform clauses.

title: Contract Terms and Conditions Required to Implement Statutes or Executive Orders—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: Master commercial flow-down clause. Lists statutorily required clauses that must flow down to commercial subcontracts (the commercial complement to 52.244-6).

title: Instructions to Offerors—Competitive Acquisition
summary: Standard Section L instructions for negotiated competitive acquisitions: late offers, withdrawal, oral presentations, contract awards.

title: Audit and Records—Negotiation
summary: Contractor must maintain records and permit government audit; flows down to cost-reimbursement and T&M subcontracts.

title: Notice of HUBZone Set-Aside or Sole Source Award
summary: Set-aside restricted to certified HUBZone small businesses. Offeror must be SBA-certified HUBZone at offer and award.

title: Notice of Price Evaluation Preference for HUBZone Small Business Concerns
summary: 10% price evaluation preference for HUBZone small businesses in unrestricted full-and-open competitions.

title: Notice of Total Small Business Set-Aside
summary: Acquisition restricted to small business concerns. Offeror must qualify as small under the assigned NAICS code's size standard.

title: Utilization of Small Business Concerns
summary: Contractor must use small businesses, including HUBZone, SDVOSB, WOSB, and 8(a) firms, to the maximum practicable extent.

title: Small Business Subcontracting Plan
summary: Other-than-small contractors with awards >$750K (>$1.5M for construction) must submit and execute a small business subcontracting plan with measurable goals.

title: Limitations on Subcontracting
summary: On set-aside contracts: services — prime cannot subcontract more than 50%; supplies — 50% of cost of manufacturing; construction — 85% (general) or 75% (specialty trade).

title: Section 8(a) Award
summary: Award restricted to 8(a) program participants. SBA holds the contract and subcontracts performance to the 8(a) firm.

title: Notice of Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Set-Aside
summary: Set-aside for service-disabled veteran-owned small businesses. Offeror must be SBA-certified SDVOSB at offer and award.

title: Post-Award Small Business Program Rerepresentation
summary: Contractor must rerepresent its size status before exercise of an option, after a merger/acquisition, or every five years.

title: Notice of Set-Aside for Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Business
summary: Set-aside for SBA-certified Economically Disadvantaged Women-Owned Small Businesses.

title: Notice of Set-Aside for Women-Owned Small Business
summary: Set-aside for SBA-certified Women-Owned Small Businesses in eligible NAICS codes.

title: Nonmanufacturer Rule
summary: On set-aside supply contracts a small business reseller must supply the product of a small business manufacturer (or have a waiver).

title: Convict Labor
summary: Contractor cannot use federal-prison-made products unless authorized.

title: Construction Wage Rate Requirements
summary: Davis-Bacon Act: construction contractors must pay locally prevailing wages. Wage determination from sam.gov is incorporated.

title: Prohibition of Segregated Facilities
summary: Contractor cannot maintain or provide segregated facilities.

title: Equal Opportunity
summary: Executive Order 11246 EEO clause. Required for awards >$10K. Flows down to subcontracts >$10K.

title: Service Contract Labor Standards
summary: Service Contract Act: services contractors must pay locally prevailing wages and fringe benefits per the wage determination.

title: Combating Trafficking in Persons
summary: Contractor and subs must not engage in trafficking activities. Compliance plan required for awards >$500K outside the U.S.

title: Buy American—Supplies
summary: Only domestic end products may be supplied unless an exception applies. Iron, steel, and manufactured-end-product cost-of-components thresholds apply.

title: Buy American—Free Trade Agreements—Israeli Trade Act
summary: Domestic or designated-country end products only. Lists countries qualifying under FTAs and the Israeli Trade Act.

title: Restrictions on Certain Foreign Purchases
summary: Cannot acquire items from sources subject to sanctions (OFAC list).

title: Rights in Data—General
summary: Government acquires unlimited rights in data first produced under the contract. Limited and restricted rights available under specific conditions.

title: Cost Accounting Standards
summary: Contractor must comply with all 19 Cost Accounting Standards if award is CAS-covered (>$2M and not exempt). Disclosure statement required for full coverage.

title: Disclosure and Consistency of Cost Accounting Practices
summary: Modified CAS coverage: contractor must comply with CAS 401, 402, 405, 406 only.

title: Prompt Payment
summary: Government will pay invoices within 30 days; interest accrues on late payments.

title: Unenforceability of Unauthorized Obligations
summary: Click-through and EULA terms in commercial software unenforceable to extent they conflict with federal law.

title: Protest After Award
summary: On post-award protest, government may direct contractor to stop work; contractor entitled to costs incurred.

title: Subcontracts for Commercial Products and Commercial Services
summary: When subcontracting for commercial items, only the listed clauses (52.203-13, 52.203-19, 52.204-23, 52.204-25, 52.219-8, 52.222-21, 52.222-26, 52.222-50, 52.225-26, 52.226-6, 52.247-64) flow down — limiting commercial-sub flow-down burden.

title: Certificate of Conformance
summary: When authorized in lieu of source inspection, contractor furnishes a certificate that supplies/services conform to contract requirements.

title: Solicitation Provisions Incorporated by Reference
summary: Listed solicitation provisions are incorporated by reference with the same force as if printed in full.

title: Clauses Incorporated by Reference
summary: Listed contract clauses are incorporated by reference with the same force as if printed in full. Full text available at acquisition.gov.
